ddls:
CREATE TABLE "camera_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each camera model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the camera model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the camera model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "resolutions" (
  "resolution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resolution */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the camera model */,
  "resolution_name" TEXT /* Name of the resolution */,
  "horizontal_pixels" INTEGER /* Horizontal pixels of the resolution */,
  "vertical_pixels" INTEGER /* Vertical pixels of the resolution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resolution_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_resolutions_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES camera_models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "camera_modes" (
  "mode_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mode */,
  "mode_name" TEXT /* Name of the mode (e.g., 4, None, 5, 6) */,
  "mode_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the mode type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mode_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_camera_modes_mode_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("mode_type_id") REFERENCES mode_types ("mode_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "mode_types" (
  "mode_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mode type */,
  "mode_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the mode type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the mode type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mode_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "camera_specifications" (
  "spec_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each specification entry */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the camera model */,
  "frame_rate_increase" TEXT /* Indicates if the frame rate can be increased (Yes/No) */,
  "brightness_level_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the brightness level */,
  "changed_by" INTEGER /* User who made changes to the specification */,
  "changed_at" TEXT /* Date and time when changes were made to the specification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("spec_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "brightness_levels" (
  "brightness_level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each brightness level */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the camera model */,
  "brightness_level_name" TEXT /* Name of the brightness level */,
  "min_brightness" INTEGER /* Minimum brightness value */,
  "max_brightness" INTEGER /* Maximum brightness value */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("brightness_level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_category" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category association */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the camera model */,
  "mode_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the camera mode */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_category_mode_id FOREIGN KEY ("mode_id") REFERENCES camera_modes ("mode_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_category_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES camera_models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user's role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the role */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the camera model */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, modify) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES camera_models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the camera model being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the model */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the camera model was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, modify) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES camera_models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "camera_specification_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "spec_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the camera specification */,
  "specification_data" TEXT /* Historical specification data */,
  "changed_by" INTEGER /* User who made changes to the specification */,
  "changed_at" TEXT /* Date and time when changes were made to the specification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_camera_specification_versions_changed_by FOREIGN KEY ("changed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
